blocks.content: Course Introduction
Welcome to the future of software engineering. This course introduces you to Google Antigravity (AGV) and the Tridorian standard for Secure Agentic Development.

items.title: The Agent-First Paradigm
items.content: Shift from being a 'manual coder' to a 'Mission Control Architect'. Learn to orchestrate digital engineers securely.

items.title: Bridging the Trust Gap
items.content: Autonomous agents require strict execution policies. We eliminate the "Black Box" risk through tangible Artifacts.

blocks.content: Course Roadmap

items: **Orchestration:** Use Agent Manager to build an app.
**Disaster Recovery:** Learn to use the Panic Button.

blocks.content: Environment Setup
Before giving an AI agent the keys to your terminal, we must establish our workspace. Choose the deployment tier that best fits your machine and risk tolerance.
Choose Your Deployment Path

blocks.title: ▶ TIER 1: Easy (Direct Binary Install)
blocks.description: Best for getting up and running quickly on a personal machine.
blocks.code:
# macOS & Linux
curl -sL https://dl.google.com/agv/install.sh | bash

# Windows (Winget)
winget install Google.Antigravity

blocks.title: ▶ TIER 2: Medium (Homebrew)
blocks.description: Best for macOS/Linux developers who want centralized dependency management.
blocks.code: brew tap google/antigravity && brew install google-antigravity

blocks.title: ▶ TIER 3: Advanced (Secure Container Sandbox)
blocks.description: Best for strict isolation. Launches an ephemeral container, preventing agents from altering your host OS.
items: **Linux:** `sudo apt install docker.io` or `sudo dnf install podman`
**Hardware Lab:** If using a bluefin-wtg lab machine, skip setup.

blocks.content: Initialize Your Workspace
Open your terminal and initialize your environment. If using Tier 3, run the following to map a local volume and enter the sandbox:

mkdir -p ~/my-agv-work

docker run -it --rm --name agv-sandbox \
  --network host \
  -v ~/my-agv-work:/workspace \
  ghcr.io/bluefin-wtg-org/agv-workspace:latest

blocks.title: Verify the Toolchain
blocks.content: Once inside, verify everything is staged perfectly without installing a single package:
blocks.code:
python3 --version
agv --version
python3 -c "from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright; print('✅ Playwright is ready.')"

blocks.content: Initializing Mission Control
Configure the AGV Editor and establish strict Execution Policies for AI agents.

items.title: Create the Project:
items.content: Initialize your project folder in your active terminal:

items.title: Launch AGV:
items.content: On your Host Machine, launch the Antigravity Editor and sign in with your Google Workspace Account.

items.title: Open Workspace:
items.content: Click *Open Folder* and select the `my-agent-app` directory.

items.title: Set Guardrails:
items.content: Navigate to **Settings > Execution Policies**. Establish the following trust parameters:

sub_blocks.content:
**Terminal Execution:** Set to *Request review*.  
*Crucial: The agent must ask permission before running bash commands.*

blocks.content: Editor Mechanics & Micro-AI
Master the micro-level developer tools in AGV before moving on to fully autonomous agents.

blocks.title: Inline Completions
blocks.content: Create a new file called `calculator.py`. Inside the file, press **Cmd + I** (Mac) or **Ctrl + I** (Windows/Linux) to open the inline prompt.
blocks.prompt: Create a function to calculate the factorial of a number.
blocks.footer: Hit Enter to generate and accept the code.

blocks.title: Context Passing
blocks.content: Intentionally break the code (e.g., delete a required colon `:`). Highlight the broken function, press **Cmd + L** (or **Ctrl + L**).
blocks.prompt: What is wrong with this code?
blocks.footer: Notice how AGV automatically attaches the highlighted code block as context to the agent's side panel.

blocks.title: Explain and Fix
blocks.content: Hover your mouse over the red squiggly error line in the editor. Click the **Quick Fix** or **Explain** lightbulb icon to have the AI resolve the syntax error instantly.
Agent Orchestration & Artifacts
Shift from micro-editing to macro-orchestration. Use "Plan Mode" to safely design and execute an AI-generated web scraping application.

items: **Open Mission Control:** Press `Cmd + E` (or `Ctrl + E`) to open the Agent Manager.
**Select Model:** Choose your AI model (e.g., Gemini 3 Pro).
**Set Mode:** Set Execution Mode to **Plan Mode**.

items.title: The Prompt:
items.content: Enter the following to begin architecture:
items.prompt: Create a basic Python script using Playwright that navigates to 'news.ycombinator.com', extracts the top 5 article titles, and saves them to a file. Enforce PEP 8 styling.

items: **Review Artifact:** The agent will generate an **Implementation Plan** instead of writing code blindly.

items.title: Iterate:
items.content: Leave a comment directly on the plan artifact to adjust the course:
items.prompt: Please update the plan to ensure the output is saved specifically as a .csv file named hacker_news.csv.

items: **Approve & Code:** Click **Approve**. The agent moves to the Coding Phase. Review the line-by-line **Code Diffs** and click **Accept**.
**Execute:** The agent will attempt to run `python3 scraper.py`. Because of our strict policies, it will pause. Click **Approve**.

items.title: Verify:
items.content: Check your terminal output:

blocks.content: Visual Walkthroughs
Utilize the Antigravity Browser to allow agents to visually test and interact with web applications natively.

blocks.title: Recommended Role
blocks.content: This lab is highly recommended for frontend developers or QA engineers looking to automate end-to-end UI testing.

items.title: 1. Build a Target App
items.content: In the Agent Manager, prompt the agent:
items.prompt: Create a simple index.html file with a Todo List app using standard JavaScript. Include an input field, an 'Add' button, and a list.

items.title: 2. Serve the App
items.content: Run a local python server in your terminal:

items.title: 3. Prompt the Visual Test
items.content: Open a new agent session and prompt:
items.prompt: Navigate to http://localhost:8000. Open the Antigravity Browser, type 'Buy groceries' into the input field, click Add, and verify the item appears in the list.

items.title: 4. Review the Tape
items.content: The agent will execute the test. Once finished, click on the **Browser Recording Artifact** in the chat history. You can actually watch a video playback of the agent clicking and typing on your application!

blocks.content: Rules vs. Workflows
Codify corporate standards using Rules, and automate repetitive tasks using Workflows to scale your productivity.

items.title: Rules (Always-On)
items.content: Rules dictate how an agent behaves globally in the background without needing to be prompted every time.

items.title: Workflows (On-Demand)
items.content: Workflows are slash-commands (e.g. `/test`) you trigger manually for specific, complex repetitive tasks.

blocks.content: 1. Create a Rule
Create the hidden directory and a rule file in your terminal:

mkdir -p .agents/rules

cat << 'EOF' > .agents/rules/style.md
Always prepend the text `# Copyright 2026 Tridorian` as a comment at the top of any new source file you generate.
EOF
2. Create a Workflow
Create a custom slash-command to generate tests automatically:

mkdir -p .agents/workflows

cat << 'EOF' > .agents/workflows/generate-tests.md
Analyze the active file and generate a comprehensive suite of unit tests using `pytest`. Save them in a `tests/` directory.
EOF

blocks.title: Test it Out!
blocks.content: Open the Agent Manager. Type `/generate-tests` in the chat. The agent will read your workflow prompt, generate the tests for your active file, AND automatically apply the Copyright header based on your global Rule!
Disaster Recovery
Learn how to safely recover from catastrophic AI hallucinations based on your deployment tier.

blocks.title: The Scenario
blocks.content: Let's say you told an agent in "Fast Mode" to clean up your directories, and it hallucinated, deleting your core system binaries. In a normal OS, you are in trouble.
1. Break the Environment
Intentionally delete core system binaries:

rm -rf $(which agv)
rm -rf $(which python3)
Verify failure by running `python3 --version`. It will fail. **Don't panic.**
2. The Panic Button

items.title: Tier 1 (Direct Install)
items.content: Rerun the setup script or winget command from Module 2.

items.title: Tier 2 (Homebrew)
items.content: Run `brew reinstall google-antigravity python`.

items.title: Tier 3 (Container Sandbox)
items.content: Type `exit` to kill the container. Press the Up Arrow in your host terminal to re-run your `docker run` command. The broken state is deleted instantly, and a fresh container is spun up in seconds.

blocks.content: Notice your source code in `my-agent-app` remains completely safe regardless of tier because it is mapped to a persistent volume!
You have successfully mastered Google Antigravity. You've transitioned from coding manually to securely orchestrating AI agents, utilizing visual browsers, and automating tasks with custom workflows.

items.title: Kubernetes Integration
items.content: Try spinning up a local cluster using Docker Desktop or `kind` on your host machine. Your agent can safely deploy pods to this cluster to test microservices locally.

items.title: Explore the Marketplace
items.content: Check out the community-driven Rules and Workflows available for AGV to speed up your specific technology stack.
